title: Sling racing
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Image}}
'''Sling racing''' was a sport that involved lightweight, [[Sling racer|modified]] [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] navigating through mountains and canyons at break-neck speeds. It gained popularity after the outlawing of [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]].
==Description==
Sling racing involved lightweight (200 [[kilogram/Legends|kg]]) [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]], the [[sling racer]]s, each having as much as 6 small repulsorlift drive units individually throttled. The speeders however were maneuverable with wing and airfooil mechanics.<ref name="guide" />

Sling racing took place among dangerous and narrow canyons, the vehicle had to take advantage of the canyon winds; the pilot adjusted the wings and foils accordingly to the currents as well as individually throttle the drives.<ref name="guide" />

The challenge involved the crudeness of controls, the requirement of predicting wind currents and the reflexes of responding to them. Rookies usually couldn't complete one course.<ref name="guide" />

==History==
Sling racing was known since before the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], and became more popular while pordracing declined and eventually was outlawed.<ref>{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|sports/13328_1.html|Podracing Faces Uncertain Future}}</ref>

Around [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]]<ref>The ''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]'' mentions that the spor was imported to [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] "a decade ago". According to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', the events of ''[[Shadows of the Empire]]'' occurred in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], therefore it reached the planet in 6 BBY or a bit earlier.</ref> the sport was introduced to [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]]; subsequently, thanks to high sponsorships and media attention, it became a cultural hype during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Civil War]].<ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

Sling racing involved wagering, with top races having over 100 entries. A few races included artificial obstacles (pillars, [[energy field/Legends|energy field]]s or [[repulsor mine]]s). There were easier courses available for amateur pilots.<ref name="guide" />

In the height of the Civil War, [[Tiaran'grast]] was one of the newest and most challenging courses.<ref name="guide" />
{{Stub}}
